title: Hepetis claussenii
taxonomy: Hepetis claussenii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It belongs to the genus Hepetis, which is a small group of plants within the Lamiaceae family. The plant's classification can be broken down into its kingdom (Plantae), division (Magnoliophyta), class (Magnoliopsida), order (Lamiales), family (Lamiaceae), genus (Hepetis), and species (H. claussenii). This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its leaf structure and flower arrangement. The classification helps to identify and distinguish Hepetis claussenii from other plants in the same family and genus.
uses: Hepetis claussenii, also known as Brazilian feather top, has been used in traditional medicine in Brazil and other parts of South America. Its leaves and stems are said to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal uses.
